key_insight: Constrained Hebbian learning reallocates limited synaptic resources to produce lower-cost representations that preserve task-relevant information under structural sparsity constraints.
engineering_relevance: For resource-limited AI and neuromorphic systems, this suggests a practical learning rule for efficiency-aware representation design rather than maximizing raw accuracy alone.
method: Compared an excitatory competitive Hebbian rule against sparse backpropagation and DDTP.
Used fixed audiovisual embeddings from AVE, Kinetics-Sounds, and VGGSound100 to isolate associative plasticity.
Evaluated representational cost with mutual-information-based CTI metrics from the Variational Information Bottleneck.
evidence_signal: Across three audiovisual benchmarks, Hebbian learning achieved lower task-information cost than sparse BP and DDTP in the main compressed comparisons, while maintaining comparable functional performance in several settings.
watch_item: The approach can slightly reduce classification accuracy on some datasets, so its benefit depends on whether cost-efficiency is prioritized over peak performance.
paper_title: Constrained Hebbian Learning Supports Efficient Representational Allocation under Structural Constraints
